title: Semantic Graph
description: Schema for the semantic graph produced by latex_to_graph.py. Represents a LaTeX expression as a directed graph of typed nodes (variables, operators, numbers) connected by edges.

nodes.description: Ordered list of graph nodes. Each node represents a symbol, operator, number, or composite expression.

edges.description: Directed edges connecting nodes. Direction flows from operand to operator (e.g. m → multiply).

domain.description: The mathematical or scientific domain of this expression (e.g. "thermodynamics", "electromagnetism", "linear_algebra", "topology"). Declared once for the whole graph; disambiguates symbols like P (power vs pressure) and informs how variables should be interpreted.

nodeType.description: The semantic role of the node.

operatorOp.description: The mathematical operation this operator or function performs.

id.description: Unique node identifier. Variables use their symbol name (e.g. "m"); generated nodes use a prefix with counter (e.g. "__multiply_1").

label.description: Human-readable descriptive name (e.g. "mass", "acceleration"). Present on symbols and numbers.

emoji.description: Emoji representing the physical or mathematical concept (e.g. "⚖️" for mass).

latex.description: LaTeX rendering of the symbol (e.g. "\\psi", "\\hbar"). Present on symbols; may differ from the id when the symbol has a special LaTeX command.

op.description: Operation name for operator, function, relation, and expression nodes. Standard operators use values from the operatorOp enum; expressions use the SymPy class name (e.g. "Tuple"); relations use the relation name (e.g. "equals", "greater_than").

exponent.description: Literal exponent value, present only on power operators with a constant exponent (e.g. "2" for x²). The exponent is absorbed into the operator node rather than represented as a separate number child.

with_respect_to.description: Differentiation variable, present only on derivative operators (e.g. "t" for d/dt).

subexpr.description: LaTeX sub-expression reconstructed by sympy.latex() for the SymPy expression rooted at this node. Used for interactive hover tooltips.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this node represents in context (e.g. "rest mass times speed of light squared — the mass-energy equivalence term"). Can be author-provided via overrides or AI-generated.

quantity.description: Physical quantity from a controlled vocabulary (e.g. "pressure", "velocity", "energy"). Enables semantic matching — two variables with the same quantity represent the same physical concept regardless of symbol name.

dimension.description: SI dimensional formula using the 7 base dimension symbols: M (mass), L (length), T (time), I (electric current), Θ (temperature), N (amount of substance), J (luminous intensity). Use '1' for dimensionless. Exponents use Unicode superscripts (e.g. "M·L⁻¹·T⁻²" for pressure, "L·T⁻¹" for velocity). Separator is middle dot '·'.

unit.description: Default SI unit (e.g. "Pa", "m/s", "kg"). Informational; does not imply the expression is in these units.

value.description: Known numerical value for constants (e.g. 299792458, "6.674e-11"). Use string for values that need scientific notation or exact representation.

role.description: The role this variable plays in the equation. Distinguishes constants from variables, independent from dependent quantities, etc.

color.description: Optional presentation override for the node's text color (any CSS color, e.g. "#0d47a1"). When present, the mermaid renderer prefers this over the role-based palette. Also used by the server-side highlight overlay to mirror step highlight colors onto matched nodes.

highlight.description: Name of the proof-step highlight that matched this node — the key used in ``proofStep.highlights`` (e.g. "m" for the highlight authored as ``\htmlClass{hl-m}{...}``). Informational only at render time; reserved for downstream visualizations that want to cross-reference graph nodes with their originating highlight.

variant.description: Optional proportionality variant for the node, mirroring the edge ``semantic`` values (direct, inverse, neutral).

label.description: Optional edge label displayed on the diagram.

semantic.description: Proportionality of the edge: direct (more input → more output), inverse (more input → less output), or neutral.

weight.description: Strength of the relationship. For a base→power edge, this is the absolute value of the exponent (e.g. 2 for x², 3 for x⁻³). Renderers multiply this by a base stroke width and clamp to [1, 8] so very large exponents stay visually sane. Omit for edges where strength is undefined (addition, structural).

kind.description: Expression type: algebraic (no derivatives), ODE (single independent variable), or PDE (multiple independent variables).

order.description: Highest derivative order. Present only for ODE/PDE.

dependent_variables.description: Variables being differentiated (e.g. ["psi"]). Present only for ODE/PDE.

independent_variables.description: Variables differentiated with respect to (e.g. ["t", "x"]). Present only for ODE/PDE.

sympy_hints.description: SymPy classify_ode hint strings (e.g. ["separable", "1st_linear"]). Present only for single-variable ODEs.

linear.description: Whether the ODE is linear. Present only for single-variable ODEs.

homogeneous.description: Whether the ODE is homogeneous. Present only for single-variable ODEs.

constant_coefficients.description: Whether the ODE has constant coefficients. Present only for single-variable ODEs.
